glycyrrhizin is a triterpenoid saponin a.name:
Glycyrrhizin is a triterpenoid saponin, a type of natural compound found in the roots and rhizomes of the licorice plant. It is relatively insoluble in water and has a bitter taste.

Glycyrrhizinate, on the other hand, is the sodium salt of glycyrrhizin, making it more soluble in water. This modified form is often used in pharmaceutical applications due to its increased bioavailability and reduced gastrointestinal side effects.

chalcones xanthoangelol xa and 4 hydroxy.name:
Chalcones: Xanthoangelol (XA) and 4-hydroxyderricin (4HD) are major polyphenolic compounds with antihypertensive, anti-obesity, and antidiabetic properties [4].

Sesquiterpenes: Ashitabaol A and other sesquiterpenes contribute to antioxidant effects and free radical scavenging activity [3].

Other Bioactives: Ashitaba also contains polyacetylenes, triterpenes, and cyclohexenones, each potentially offering medicinal benefits [3].
chalcones xanthoangelol xa and 4 hydroxy.compound:

Sesquiterpenes: Ashitabaol A and other sesquiterpenes contribute to antioxidant effects and free radical scavenging activity [3].
